title: Automatically sensing a type of charging cable and setting maximum amperage output of an electric vehicle charging station accordingly
abstract: An electric vehicle charging station detects a property of a charging cable connected to the electric vehicle charging station and determines, based on the detected property, an ampere capacity of the charging cable. The electric vehicle charging station automatically sets a maximum amperage output of the electric vehicle charging station to not exceed the determined ampere capacity of the charging cable.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method in an electric vehicle charging station, comprising: detecting, by the electric vehicle charging station, a property of a charging cable connected to the electric vehicle charging station; determining, by the electric vehicle charging station, an ampere capacity of the charging cable based on the detected property; and automatically setting, by the electric vehicle charging station, a maximum amperage output of the electric vehicle charging station to not exceed the determined ampere capacity of the charging cable. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein detecting the property of the charging cable includes the electric vehicle charging station detecting whether a magnet is included in the charging cable, and wherein determining the ampere capacity of the charging cable is determined based on whether a magnet was detected in the charging cable. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the property of the charging cable is the absence of a magnet or presence of a magnet. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein detecting the property of the charging cable includes detecting whether the charging cable interrupts a light sensor. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein detecting the property of the charging cable includes reading an RFID tag included in the charging cable that identifies a type of the charging cable. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein automatically setting the maximum amperage output of the electric vehicle charging station includes signaling a maximum available continuous current capacity that does not exceed the determined ampere capacity of the charging cable to an electric vehicle connected to the electric vehicle charging station via the charging cable. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein signaling the maximum available continuous current capacity is performed through pulse width modulation of a control pilot signal. 8 An electric vehicle charging station, compri a sensor configured to detect a property of a charging cable connected to the electric vehicle charging station; a processing system coupled with the sensor and configured to determine an ampere capacity of the charging cable based on the detected property; and control electronics coupled with the processing system to automatically set a maximum amperage output of the electric vehicle charging station to not exceed the determined ampere capacity of the charging cable. 9. The electric vehicle charging system of claim 8, wherein the sensor is a hall effect sensor that is configured to detect the presence of a magnet included in a predefined location of the charging cable. 10. The electric vehicle charging system of claim 8, wherein the sensor is configured to detect whether the charging cable interrupts a light. 11. The electric vehicle charging system of claim 8, wherein the sensor is configured to read an RFID tag included in the charging cable that identifies a type of the charging cable. 12. The electric vehicle charging system of claim 8, wherein the automatically setting of the maximum amperage output of the electric vehicle charging station includes the control electronics to signal a maximum available continuous current capacity that does not exceed the determined ampere capacity of the charging cable to an electric vehicle connected to the electric vehicle charging station via the charging cable. 13. The electric vehicle charging system of claim 12, wherein the control electronics is to signal the maximum available continuous current capacity through pulse width modulation of a control pilot signal. 14. A method in an electric vehicle charging station, comprising: detecting, by the electric vehicle charging station, that a magnet is included in a first charging cable connected to the electric vehicle charging station; responsive to determining that the magnet is included in the first charging cable, determining a first ampere capacity of the first charging cable; automatically setting, by the electric vehicle charging station, a maximum amperage output to not exceed the determined first ampere capacity of the first charging cable; detecting, by the electric vehicle charging station, that a magnet is not included in a second charging cable connected to the electric vehicle charging station; responsive to determining that the magnet is not included in the second charging cable, determining a second ampere capacity of the second charging cable; and automatically setting, by the electric vehicle charging station, a maximum amperage output to not exceed the determined second ampere capacity of the second charging cable. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the detecting that the magnet is included in the first charging cable is by a hall effect sensor included in the electric vehicle charging station. 16. The method of claim 14, wherein automatically setting the maximum amperage output of the electric vehicle charging station to not exceed the determined first ampere capacity of the first charging cable includes signaling a first maximum available continuous current capacity that does not exceed the determined first ampere capacity of the first charging cable to a first electric vehicle connected to the electric vehicle charging station via the first charging cable, and 

 wherein automatically setting the maximum amperage output of the electric vehicle charging station to not exceed the determined second ampere capacity of the second charging cable includes signaling a second maximum available continuous current capacity that does not exceed the determined second ampere capacity of the second charging cable to a second electric vehicle connected to the electric vehicle charging station via the second charging cable. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein signaling the first maximum available continuous current capacity and signaling the second maximum available continuous current capacity is performed through pulse width modulation of a control pilot signal. 18. The method of claim 14, wherein the first ampere capacity is greater than the second ampere capacity. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the first ampere capacity is 32 Amps and the second ampere capacity is 16 Amps.
